note: Interaction table keyed by salt (generic) names in lowercase. Severity: major = avoid combination / needs doctor review, moderate = use with caution / monitor.

interactions.effect: Greatly increased bleeding risk when an anticoagulant is combined with an antiplatelet.
interactions.advice: Do not combine without explicit cardiologist guidance. Watch for unusual bruising or black stools.

interactions.effect: Azithromycin can raise warfarin levels (INR), increasing bleeding risk.
interactions.advice: INR should be monitored closely if both are prescribed.

interactions.effect: Metronidazole strongly potentiates warfarin, causing dangerous INR elevation.
interactions.advice: Combination usually avoided; requires dose adjustment and INR monitoring.

interactions.effect: NSAIDs with warfarin sharply increase gastrointestinal bleeding risk.
interactions.advice: Avoid NSAIDs; paracetamol is the safer pain option with warfarin.

interactions.effect: NSAIDs with warfarin sharply increase gastrointestinal bleeding risk.
interactions.advice: Avoid NSAIDs; paracetamol is the safer pain option with warfarin.

interactions.effect: NSAIDs with warfarin sharply increase gastrointestinal bleeding risk.
interactions.advice: Avoid NSAIDs; paracetamol is the safer pain option with warfarin.

interactions.effect: NSAIDs with warfarin sharply increase gastrointestinal bleeding risk.
interactions.advice: Avoid NSAIDs; paracetamol is the safer pain option with warfarin.

interactions.effect: Dual antiplatelet therapy increases bleeding risk. Often intentional after stents, but must be doctor-directed.
interactions.advice: Confirm the combination is intentional (e.g. post-angioplasty). Report unusual bleeding.

interactions.effect: Omeprazole reduces activation of clopidogrel, weakening its anti-clotting effect.
interactions.advice: Pantoprazole is the preferred acid reducer with clopidogrel.

interactions.effect: Ibuprofen can block low-dose aspirin's heart-protective effect and adds GI bleeding risk.
interactions.advice: Separate dosing times or use paracetamol instead; consult a doctor.

interactions.effect: Two NSAID-type drugs together increase gastric bleeding and ulcer risk.
interactions.advice: Avoid combining; add a gastro-protective agent if unavoidable.

interactions.effect: Duplicate NSAID therapy — no added benefit, higher ulcer and kidney risk.
interactions.advice: Use only one NSAID at a time.

interactions.effect: Duplicate NSAID therapy — no added benefit, higher ulcer and kidney risk.
interactions.advice: Use only one NSAID at a time.

interactions.effect: Duplicate NSAID therapy — no added benefit, higher ulcer and kidney risk.
interactions.advice: Use only one NSAID at a time.

interactions.effect: Risk of serotonin syndrome (agitation, high temperature, rapid heart rate) and lowered seizure threshold.
interactions.advice: Combination needs medical supervision; seek help for tremor, confusion or fever.

interactions.effect: Risk of serotonin syndrome and lowered seizure threshold.
interactions.advice: Combination needs medical supervision; seek help for tremor, confusion or fever.

interactions.effect: Combined CNS depression — excessive sedation, impaired breathing, falls.
interactions.advice: Avoid taking together; never combine with alcohol.

interactions.effect: Opioid + benzodiazepine combination can dangerously suppress breathing.
interactions.advice: Avoid unless specifically directed and monitored by a doctor.

interactions.effect: Both can prolong the QT interval; together they raise the risk of serious heart rhythm disturbance.
interactions.advice: Caution in heart patients or with other QT-prolonging drugs.

interactions.effect: Fluoroquinolones with NSAIDs can increase risk of seizures and tendon problems.
interactions.advice: Prefer paracetamol for pain while on ciprofloxacin.

interactions.effect: ACE inhibitor + ARB dual blockade raises risk of kidney injury and high potassium.
interactions.advice: These two classes are generally not combined; confirm with the prescriber.

interactions.effect: ACE inhibitor + ARB dual blockade raises risk of kidney injury and high potassium.
interactions.advice: These two classes are generally not combined; confirm with the prescriber.

interactions.effect: NSAIDs blunt blood-pressure control and, with ACE inhibitors, stress the kidneys.
interactions.advice: Limit NSAID use; monitor blood pressure and kidney function.

interactions.effect: NSAIDs blunt blood-pressure control and can stress the kidneys with ARBs.
interactions.advice: Limit NSAID use; prefer paracetamol.

interactions.effect: Fluoroquinolones can disturb blood sugar (both high and low episodes) in diabetics.
interactions.advice: Monitor blood glucose more frequently during the antibiotic course.

interactions.effect: Macrolide antibiotics can raise statin levels, increasing risk of muscle injury.
interactions.advice: Report unexplained muscle pain or dark urine; short courses are usually tolerated.

interactions.effect: Calcium binds thyroxine in the gut and reduces its absorption.
interactions.advice: Separate doses by at least 4 hours (thyroxine on empty stomach in the morning).

interactions.effect: Iron salts reduce thyroxine absorption.
interactions.advice: Separate doses by at least 4 hours.

interactions.effect: Ondansetron may reduce tramadol's pain relief; both affect serotonin.
interactions.advice: Watch for inadequate pain control; discuss alternatives with the doctor.

interactions.effect: PDE5 inhibitors with nitrates cause severe, potentially fatal blood-pressure drop.
interactions.advice: Never combine. Seek emergency care if chest pain occurs after taking both.

interactions.effect: PDE5 inhibitors with nitrates cause severe, potentially fatal blood-pressure drop.
interactions.advice: Never combine. Seek emergency care if chest pain occurs after taking both.

interactions.effect: PDE5 inhibitors with nitrates cause severe, potentially fatal blood-pressure drop.
interactions.advice: Never combine. Seek emergency care if chest pain occurs after taking both.

interactions.effect: PDE5 inhibitors with nitrates cause severe, potentially fatal blood-pressure drop.
interactions.advice: Never combine. Seek emergency care if chest pain occurs after taking both.

interactions.effect: NSAIDs reduce methotrexate clearance, risking serious toxicity (mouth ulcers, low blood counts).
interactions.advice: Avoid regular NSAID use on methotrexate; consult the rheumatologist.

interactions.effect: NSAIDs reduce methotrexate clearance, risking serious toxicity.
interactions.advice: Avoid regular NSAID use on methotrexate; consult the rheumatologist.

interactions.effect: NSAIDs reduce methotrexate clearance, risking serious toxicity.
interactions.advice: Avoid regular NSAID use on methotrexate; consult the rheumatologist.

interactions.effect: Aspirin reduces methotrexate clearance, risking serious toxicity.
interactions.advice: Avoid combining without specialist guidance.

interactions.effect: Clarithromycin blocks colchicine breakdown — reported fatal colchicine toxicity.
interactions.advice: Combination should be avoided; ask the doctor for an alternative antibiotic.

interactions.effect: Clarithromycin sharply raises statin levels — risk of muscle breakdown (rhabdomyolysis).
interactions.advice: Statin is usually paused during the clarithromycin course.

interactions.effect: Clarithromycin can raise statin exposure — muscle injury risk.
interactions.advice: Report muscle pain or dark urine promptly.

interactions.effect: Statin + fibrate increases risk of muscle injury.
interactions.advice: Often co-prescribed deliberately — report unexplained muscle pain.

interactions.effect: Statin + fibrate increases risk of muscle injury.
interactions.advice: Often co-prescribed deliberately — report unexplained muscle pain.

interactions.effect: Fluconazole raises statin levels — muscle injury risk.
interactions.advice: Usually fine for single-dose fluconazole; caution with longer courses.

interactions.effect: Fluconazole strongly potentiates warfarin — bleeding risk.
interactions.advice: INR must be monitored; dose adjustment often needed.

interactions.effect: Both prolong the QT interval — heart rhythm risk.
interactions.advice: Avoid combining in heart patients; watch for palpitations or fainting.

interactions.effect: Ciprofloxacin massively raises tizanidine levels — severe drowsiness and BP drop.
interactions.advice: Contraindicated combination; ask for a different antibiotic or muscle relaxant.

interactions.effect: Ciprofloxacin raises theophylline levels — nausea, tremor, seizures possible.
interactions.advice: Needs dose reduction and monitoring; tell the doctor you take Deriphyllin.

interactions.effect: ACE inhibitor + potassium-sparing diuretic can cause dangerous potassium rise.
interactions.advice: Common combo in heart failure but needs periodic potassium blood tests.

interactions.effect: ARB + potassium-sparing diuretic can cause dangerous potassium rise.
interactions.advice: Needs periodic potassium blood tests.

interactions.effect: ARB + potassium-sparing diuretic can cause dangerous potassium rise.
interactions.advice: Needs periodic potassium blood tests.

interactions.effect: Amiodarone raises digoxin levels — toxicity (nausea, vision changes, arrhythmia).
interactions.advice: Digoxin dose is usually halved; levels must be monitored.

interactions.effect: Clarithromycin raises digoxin levels — toxicity risk.
interactions.advice: Monitor for nausea, confusion, palpitations; inform the doctor.

interactions.effect: Triptan + SSRI: small risk of serotonin syndrome.
interactions.advice: Watch for agitation, sweating, tremor after dosing together.

interactions.effect: Triptan + SSRI: small risk of serotonin syndrome.
interactions.advice: Watch for agitation, sweating, tremor after dosing together.

interactions.effect: SSRIs + NSAIDs increase stomach bleeding risk.
interactions.advice: Prefer paracetamol; add a PPI if NSAID is unavoidable.

interactions.effect: SSRIs + NSAIDs increase stomach bleeding risk.
interactions.advice: Prefer paracetamol; add a PPI if NSAID is unavoidable.

interactions.effect: SSRIs + aspirin increase stomach bleeding risk.
interactions.advice: Often co-prescribed; watch for black stools, add gastro-protection.

interactions.effect: Calcium binds doxycycline and blocks its absorption.
interactions.advice: Separate doses by at least 2–3 hours.

interactions.effect: Iron binds doxycycline and blocks its absorption.
interactions.advice: Separate doses by at least 2–3 hours.

interactions.effect: Calcium binds ciprofloxacin and reduces its effect.
interactions.advice: Take the antibiotic 2 hours before or 6 hours after calcium.

interactions.effect: Iron binds ciprofloxacin and reduces its effect.
interactions.advice: Take the antibiotic 2 hours before or 6 hours after iron.

interactions.effect: Calcium binds levofloxacin and reduces its effect.
interactions.advice: Separate doses by several hours.

interactions.effect: Both prolong the QT interval — heart rhythm risk.
interactions.advice: Caution in cardiac patients; ECG monitoring if combined long-term.

interactions.effect: Risk of serotonin syndrome and lowered seizure threshold.
interactions.advice: Combination needs medical supervision.

interactions.effect: Risk of serotonin syndrome and lowered seizure threshold.
interactions.advice: Combination needs medical supervision.

interactions.effect: Combined CNS depression — excessive sedation, impaired breathing, falls.
interactions.advice: Avoid taking together; never combine with alcohol.

interactions.effect: Combined CNS depression — excessive sedation, impaired breathing, falls.
interactions.advice: Avoid taking together; never combine with alcohol.

interactions.effect: Clarithromycin raises carbamazepine to toxic levels (dizziness, double vision).
interactions.advice: Needs level monitoring or an alternative antibiotic.

interactions.effect: Fluconazole raises phenytoin levels — toxicity risk.
interactions.advice: Levels should be monitored if combined.

interactions.effect: Carbapenem antibiotics drastically drop valproate levels — seizure risk.
interactions.advice: Combination generally avoided.

interactions.effect: Long-term acid suppression can reduce thyroxine absorption.
interactions.advice: Take thyroxine on an empty stomach; recheck TSH if PPI is chronic.

interactions.effect: Steroids + NSAIDs multiply stomach ulcer and bleeding risk.
interactions.advice: Avoid casual NSAID use on steroids; add a PPI if needed.

interactions.effect: Steroids + NSAIDs multiply stomach ulcer and bleeding risk.
interactions.advice: Avoid casual NSAID use on steroids; add a PPI if needed.

interactions.effect: Loop diuretics can affect kidney function and metformin clearance.
interactions.advice: Kidney function should be checked periodically.

interactions.effect: Higher chance of skin rash when combined.
interactions.advice: Report any rash promptly.

interactions.effect: Both stress the liver; several combos double-dose paracetamol unknowingly.
interactions.advice: Avoid alcohol; do not exceed 3g paracetamol/day in total.
